items.setting: You receive an email from your university inviting you to join a research study about music preferences. You have scheduled a short online interview with a researcher to answer a few questions.
questions: First, how often do you listen to music?
Do you listen daily, weekly or less often?
What genres or types of music do you usually enjoy listening to?
For example, do you prefer classical, rock or jazz music?
Can you tell me about a concert or live music event you would like to attend?
Why would you make that particular choice?
Do you agree or disagree with this viewpoint?
Why?
items.transcript: . First, how often do you listen to music? Do you listen daily, weekly or less often? What genres or types of music do you usually enjoy listening to? For example, do you prefer classical, rock or jazz music? Give details to explain your answer. Can you tell me about a concert or live music event you would like to attend? Why would you make that particular choice? me people believe that music can have a powerful effect on our emotions and moods. Do you agree or disagree with this viewpoint? Why?

questions: First, do you have any specific routines or practices you use to maintain your physical well-being?
If so, what are they?
Can you describe any dietary choices or habits that you follow to stay healthy?
If you could make one significant change to your diet or exercise routine to stay healthy, what would it be?
Why would you make that particular choice?
Do you agree or disagree with this viewpoint?
Why?
items.transcript: . First, do you have any specific routines or practices you use to maintain your physical well-being? If so, what are they? Can you describe any dietary choices or habits that you follow to stay healthy? Give details to explain your answer. If you could make one significant change to your diet or exercise routine to stay healthy, what would it be? Why would you make that particular choice? me people believe that mental health is just as important as physical health and should be given equal attention. Do you agree or disagree with this viewpoint? Why?

questions: First, do you think exercising daily is important for good health?
Why or why not?
What kind of exercise or fitness routine would you recommend?
Why?
For that same friend or family member, what do you think might challenge them the most about a regular exercise program?
How would you help them through this challenge?
What do you think would be a good reward for the program you created and why?
items.transcript: . for agreeing to participate. First, do you think exercising daily is important for good health? Why or why not? Imagine you are going to recommend or create an exercise program for a friend or family member who does not currently exercise. What kind of exercise or fitness routine would you recommend? Why? For that same friend or family member, what do you think might challenge them the most about a regular exercise program? How would you help them through this challenge? me people believe that it is important to have some sort of reward after a good week of exercising. What do you think would be a good reward for the program you created and why?

questions: First, did you enjoy your school experience as a child?
What made that project or activity special?
Do you feel like that technology prepared you for life after school?
Why or why not?
Do you agree or disagree with this viewpoint?
Why?
items.transcript: . for agreeing to participate. First, did you enjoy your school experience as a child? Tell me about a school project or activity you participated in. What made that project or activity special? Tell me a little about the technology you used during your childhood education. Do you feel like that technology prepared you for life after school? Why or why not? me people believe that the current education system needs significant changes to better prepare children for the future, focusing more on skills like problem solving and teamwork, instead of mainly teaching facts. Do you agree or disagree with this viewpoint? Why?

items.setting: You have signed up for a study run by a university research group that is investigating education experiences. You will have a short video interview with one of the researchers.
questions: First, which do you think is more important for a college?
Offering a wide variety of courses or having small class sizes?
Would you prefer to study by yourself or with the group?
What would make you choose one over the other?
What are your thoughts on this?
Do you agree or disagree?
Why?
Do you think online learning will someday completely replace in-person learning?
items.transcript: . for joining the study. Today, I'd like to ask you some questions about your education experiences. First, which do you think is more important for a college? Offering a wide variety of courses or having small class sizes? Imagine that you are preparing for a test. Would you prefer to study by yourself or with the group? What would make you choose one over the other? me people believe that online courses offer more flexibility and accessibility compared to those offered in traditional classroom settings and are therefore superior. What are your thoughts on this? Do you agree or disagree? Why? points. Lastly, online courses are becoming increasingly popular and there are now full degree programs offered by universities where every class is available online only. Do you think online learning will someday completely replace in-person learning? Explain your thoughts.

items.setting: You received an email from your university inviting you to join a research study about entertainment preferences. You have scheduled a short online interview with a researcher to answer a few questions.
questions: What kind of movies do your family or friends generally like to watch?
For example, do they prefer action movies, comedies, dramas, or other types?
When you watch a movie, do you prefer to watch after work or school on weekdays?
Or do you like to watch during the weekends?
Why?
Do you think that movie theaters will continue to exist in the future?
Why or why not?
Do you agree with this idea?
Or do you think there are other, more effective ways to educate people?
items.transcript: me questions about your entertainment preferences. What kind of movies do your family or friends generally like to watch? For example, do they prefer action movies, comedies, dramas, or other types? When you watch a movie, do you prefer to watch after work or school on weekdays? Or do you like to watch during the weekends? Why? Next, I'd like to get your opinion. In the past, people mostly watched movies and theaters. Today, many people watch movies at home. Do you think that movie theaters will continue to exist in the future? Why or why not? points. I just have one more question. me people believe that movies can be a powerful tool for educating people and raising awareness about important issues. Do you agree with this idea? Or do you think there are other, more effective ways to educate people? Explain why you think so.

questions: To begin, do you have a hobby or interest that you regularly spend time doing?
If you were to select a new hobby, what would you choose and why?
What do you think about that and why?
items.transcript: . for agreeing to participate. To begin, do you have a hobby or interest that you regularly spend time doing? If you were to select a new hobby, what would you choose and why? tell me what might prevent you from starting this new pastime. me people believe it is better to have one interest outside of work or school that you dedicate yourself to rather than multiple smaller ones. What do you think about that and why?

questions: First, do you take any specific actions to reduce your environmental impact, such as recycling or conserving energy?
Can you describe one or two steps your community or neighborhood takes to be environmentally friendly?
For example, are there solar panels on buildings or rainwater barrels available where you live?
if you had the chance to participate in an ecological or nature-based activity, such as a community cleanup effort or tree planting event, what would you do and why?
Do you agree or disagree with this viewpoint?
Why or why not?
items.transcript: . for agreeing to participate. First, do you take any specific actions to reduce your environmental impact, such as recycling or conserving energy? Give details in your answer. Can you describe one or two steps your community or neighborhood takes to be environmentally friendly? For example, are there solar panels on buildings or rainwater barrels available where you live? if you had the chance to participate in an ecological or nature-based activity, such as a community cleanup effort or tree planting event, what would you do and why? me people believe that individual actions are not enough to address conservation issues and that significant changes must come from government policies. Do you agree or disagree with this viewpoint? Why or why not?

questions: First, how important is technology in your day-to-day activities?
Have you ever felt frustrated while using technology?
What happened?
What is your opinion and why?
Do you agree or disagree with this belief?
Why or why not?
items.transcript: . for signing up for the study. First, how important is technology in your day-to-day activities? Noted. Have you ever felt frustrated while using technology? What happened? Fair enough. Next question. me people believe technology will replace many jobs while others think it will create more opportunities. What is your opinion and why? Last question. me people believe that reliance on technology is reducing our ability to think independently and solve problems. Do you agree or disagree with this belief? Why or why not?

questions: What was it?

questions: First, what kinds of places do you usually go to during the week and how do you get there?
Can you describe how someone in your family or a friend usually travels to work or school?
How much time does it take and what transportation does the person use?
While others think location doesn't matter much, what do you think?
why?

items.transcript: . for your willingness to participate today. First, what kinds of places do you usually go to during the week and how do you get there? Can you describe how someone in your family or a friend usually travels to work or school? How much time does it take and what transportation does the person use? thanks. Next question. me people believe that living close to work or school improves quality of life. While others think location doesn't matter much, what do you think? why? Final question. me people believe that employers should reimburse employees for their commuting costs while others think that employees should pay for their own travel to work. Which view do you agree with and why?

questions: First, do you currently play any sports or engage regularly in any physical activities?
If you were able to, what sport that you do not currently play, would you like to be really good at?
Why that particular sport?
What made it memorable?
What do you think and why?
items.transcript: . for agreeing to participate. First, do you currently play any sports or engage regularly in any physical activities? If you were able to, what sport that you do not currently play, would you like to be really good at? Why that particular sport? Tell me about an experience you had while playing a sport or engaging in a physical activity that you remember well. What made it memorable? me people believe that children should not participate in contact sports like rugby or hockey, because they could get hurt, while others believe that these sports teach awareness and teamwork. What do you think and why?

items.setting: You received an email from your university inviting you to join a research study about personal finance. You have scheduled a short online interview with a researcher to answer a few questions.
questions: First, how often do you review your money management, such as tracking your expenses or budgeting?
Do you do this weekly, monthly or less often?
Can you describe one or two methods you or someone you know uses to manage your finances?
For example, do you use budgeting apps or keep a written record?
What motivated you to achieve this goal?
Do you agree or disagree with this idea?
Why?
items.transcript: . First, how often do you review your money management, such as tracking your expenses or budgeting? Do you do this weekly, monthly or less often? Can you describe one or two methods you or someone you know uses to manage your finances? For example, do you use budgeting apps or keep a written record? Tell me about a financial goal you have achieved recently. What motivated you to achieve this goal? me people believe that fiscal responsibility education should be mandatory in schools to help students manage money effectively as adults. Do you agree or disagree with this idea? Why?

questions: First, do you enjoy attending large social gatherings or do you prefer smaller, more private gatherings?
Why is that?
Can you describe a memorable social gathering you attended recently?
What made it special?
How do you usually prepare for a social gathering?
What do you focus on most?
Do you agree or disagree with this idea?
Why or why not?
items.transcript: . for being part of the study. I want to ask you some questions about your experiences with social gatherings. First, do you enjoy attending large social gatherings or do you prefer smaller, more private gatherings? Why is that? Can you describe a memorable social gathering you attended recently? What made it special? Very good. How do you usually prepare for a social gathering? What do you focus on most? me people believe that social gatherings are important opportunities for building and maintaining professional relationships. Do you agree or disagree with this idea? Why or why not?
